items.criterion: `PinnedShotGroup` type gains `start: number` and `children: { clipId, offset, duration }[]`; existing fields preserved
`projectPinnedShotGroups(config)` is called inside `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry` BEFORE `resolvedConfig` is constructed (not inside `assembleTimelineData`)

`useTimelineCommit.applyEdit` runs `projectPinnedShotGroups` on incoming config for type 'config' and type 'pinnedShotGroups' so `useClipEditing`/`useTimelineTrackManagement` callers cannot bypass projection
All three `PinnedShotGroup` clone helpers (`migrate.ts:163`, `timeline-data.ts:91`, `usePinnedShotGroups.ts:31`) deep-copy `start` and `children`
Legacy configs without `start`/`children` are upgraded inside `repairConfig` (idempotent; missing clipIds dropped; start clamped ≥ 0)
`pinned-group-projection.ts` exposes both `buildGroupFromChildren` (children-as-input primitive) and `buildGroupFromConfigClips` (config-lookup convenience wrapper)
`useExternalDrop` builds the new group inline from its loop's `timeOffset` and `nextEdit.duration`, calling `buildGroupFromChildren`; it does NOT read `workingData.config.clips` for the new clip IDs
`useTimelineState.contexts.ts` exposes a shared `interactionStateRef: { drag, resize }` and `isInteractionActive()` accessor

`useClipDrag` writes `interactionStateRef.current.drag` true/false on drag lifecycle; `TimelineCanvas` `handleResizePointerDown`/`endResize` write `interactionStateRef.current.resize` true/false symmetric with `pendingOpsRef`
`usePinnedGroupSync` checks `isInteractionActive()` and defers (re-arms debounce) when true; sync runs after the interaction ends
Drag of any clip in a pinned group is represented as a single `GroupDragEntry`; deferred expansion is removed
Outer-edge group resize goes through `resizeGroupEdge`; per-clip resize handles on group members are not rendered; interior boundary resize is rejected
`positionedShotGroups` renders bounding box from `group.start` + last child's `offset+duration`; the in-array-order contiguity check is deleted
Keyboard Backspace/Delete and `s` (split) on a selected group member are NO-OPs (group intact, no clips removed); an error toast may be shown but no mutation occurs
ArrowUp/ArrowDown and `moveSelectedClipsToTrack` on a selection containing a group member reroute to a whole-group move via `moveGroup`
Shot-group menu Delete shot still calls `handleDeleteClips(group.clipIds)` then `unpinGroup(...)`; Unpin/Deconstruct still calls only `unpinGroup`
Property test: random sequences of `moveGroup`/`resizeGroupEdge`/`replaceGroupChildren` always produce contiguous, spatially sorted children after projection

Commit-boundary integration test passes: legacy config through `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry` yields `resolvedConfig.clips` with member starts equal to `group.start + child.offset`, and `signature` reflects the projected config
Sync-deferral test passes: while `interactionStateRef.current.resize = true`, the debounced sync callback does not mutate the group; after release, the next tick applies
`npx tsc --noEmit` passes
All existing `src/tools/video-editor/` tests pass (with updates); pre-existing TimelineCanvas `data-shot-group-kind="pinned"` failure is unchanged
Group `start` is clamped ≥ 0 in every drag/resize/keyboard path via `moveGroup`/`findBestGroupStart`
Remotion preview renders the same output for an unchanged timeline (flat `clips[]` semantics unchanged)
Now-dead cohesion code removed: `getShotGroupAdjacent`, deferred-expansion fields, collective-extent calc in `resolveGroupOverlaps`, interior auto-shift in `useClipResize` for group members
Diff is concentrated in `src/tools/video-editor/` with no unrelated cleanups
Manual smoke: keyboard Delete on a group member is a visible no-op; sync deferred mid-resize; all shot label menu actions unchanged; Remotion preview unchanged
Attempting to break a pinned group via drag/resize/snap/multi-select/cross-track drag/keyboard shortcut/external drop is impossible; only shot-label-menu Deconstruct/Delete shot can break it

unresolved_flags.concern: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: Checked the actual ownership of interaction state across the repo. Resize liveness currently lives in `TimelineCanvas` (`resizeSessionRef`, `pendingOpsRef`, `resizeOverrides`) rather than in `useDragCoordinator`, so rev 2 still does not touch all supporting infrastructure needed for a reliable sync-deferral guard.
unresolved_flags.evidence: Rev 3 fixes the 3 root causes the gate consolidated from rev 2's 16 open flags, none requiring redesign of the hybrid model: (1) **Keyboard delete/split on a group member is now a NO-OP** with an error toast, not a reroute to delete-shot semantics — the brief is explicit that only the shot-label menu may break a group. ArrowUp/Down and `moveSelectedClipsToTrack` still reroute to whole-group moves because moving doesn't break cohesion. (2) **`useExternalDrop` builds the group inside its existing loop** using the `timeOffset` accumulator and `nextEdit.duration` it already tracks at `useExternalDrop.ts:148-169` — a new `buildGroupFromChildren` primitive takes a `children` array directly so the helper doesn't need to read `workingData.config.clips` (which doesn't yet contain the new clip IDs). The selection-pinning caller uses a `buildGroupFromConfigClips` convenience wrapper. (3) **Interaction state is lifted into the editor state layer** (`useTimelineState.contexts.ts`) as a shared `interactionStateRef: { drag, resize }` ref, written by both `useClipDrag` (drag lifecycle) and `TimelineCanvas` resize handlers (`resizeSessionRef`/`pendingOpsRef` at `TimelineCanvas.tsx:737-843`), read by `usePinnedGroupSync`. Rev 2 wrongly assumed `useDragCoordinator` already tracked resizes. New Step 7 makes this explicit and Step 17 adds a sync-deferral test. All other rev-2 content (projection wiring, clone helpers, Delete-shot semantics preservation, broader edit surface) is preserved.

rationale: Massive convergence: weighted score 28 → 27 → **10.75** (a 61% drop in one iteration), 16 of 18 flags resolved, plan delta 49% (focused, not churning). All seven remaining flags are the SAME concern wearing different hats: rev 3 proposed putting the shared `interactionStateRef` in `useTimelineState.contexts.ts`, but the critique correctly observes that `useClipDrag`, `TimelineCanvas`, and `usePinnedGroupSync` all already converge in `TimelineEditor.tsx` (at lines 194, 479, 663-712), so a local `useRef<{drag, resize}>` declared in `TimelineEditor` and passed as a prop/callback to those three consumers is strictly simpler — no provider/type plumbing needed. This is a **trivial execution-time decision**, not a planning problem: the executor should declare the ref in `TimelineEditor.tsx`, pass it to `useClipDrag` (or set it via a callback), pass it to `TimelineCanvas` via props for the resize handlers to set, and read it inside the `usePinnedGroupSync` debounced callback via a closure over the same ref. The *behavior* is settled (drag writes `.drag`, resize writes `.resize` symmetric with `pendingOpsRef`, sync reads `.drag || .resize` and defers). Only the *physical location* is bikeshedding, and further iteration would waste loop budget on a one-line decision. All the substantive concerns from rev 1 and rev 2 (signature staleness, clone helpers, Delete-shot semantics, keyboard delete no-op, useExternalDrop loop-based group construction, broader edit surface, projection wiring) are resolved. The escalated debt overlap exists but is historical across other plans, not recurring on THIS plan's trajectory. Resolving the top flag as accept_tradeoff with a direction, since the critique is correct and the fix is trivial.
signals_assessment: Iteration 3, weighted score 28.0 → 27.0 → 10.75 — a decisive drop indicating real convergence. Plan delta 49.4% is focused refinement, not churn. 16 of 18 original flags resolved; 0 recurring critiques. The 7 remaining flags all collapse into ONE concern (where to physically place the shared interaction-state ref), with different reviewers approaching it from correctness/scope/completeness/callers angles — none is a substantive new issue. Preflight clean. Escalated debt subsystems overlap historically but the trajectory on this plan shows the opposite of a stuck loop: each iteration resolves more than it surfaces. Further iteration would be bikeshedding a one-line implementation decision.
warnings: Execution-time decision required: the shared `interactionStateRef` should be declared as a local `useRef` in `TimelineEditor.tsx` (where `useClipDrag`, `TimelineCanvas`, and `usePinnedGroupSync` already converge at lines 194, 479, 663-712) and passed to consumers via props/callbacks — NOT lifted into `useTimelineState.contexts.ts` or the split context surface. This avoids updates to `useTimelineState.types.ts`, `TimelineEditorContext.tsx`, `VideoEditorProvider.tsx`, and the provider test. The behavior contract remains: `useClipDrag` writes `.drag` on pointerdown-after-threshold and pointerup/cancel; `TimelineCanvas` `handleResizePointerDown`/`endResize` write `.resize` symmetric with `pendingOpsRef`; `usePinnedGroupSync`'s debounced callback reads `.drag || .resize` and re-arms the debounce when true.
Watch for: the sync-deferral test (Step 17.4) must assert the re-arm behavior, not just skip-once.

Historical debt in `implement-two-tier-shot-20260406-1054` on cross-track group metadata and post-commit reconcile churn is NOT in this plan's scope (different plan, earlier iteration); don't re-litigate during execution.
During execution, verify no second materialization path rebuilds rows from raw config before committing — the projection guarantee depends on `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry` being the sole entry.

settled_decisions.decision: Hybrid: group-centric authoring (`start` + relative `children: {clipId, offset, duration}[]`) with flat `clips[]` retained as Remotion source of truth, projected via `projectPinnedShotGroups`.
settled_decisions.rationale: Cohesion-by-construction with minimal Remotion blast radius.

settled_decisions.decision: `projectPinnedShotGroups` runs in `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry` BEFORE `resolvedConfig` is constructed, AND in `useTimelineCommit.applyEdit` for `'config'`/`'pinnedShotGroups'` types. Never inside `assembleTimelineData`.
settled_decisions.rationale: Keeps `resolvedConfig`, `signature`, and rows all projected; covers both rows-path and config-path callers.

settled_decisions.decision: Interior resize and split are blocked for group members. Keyboard Delete on a group member is a NO-OP (error toast allowed). ArrowUp/Down and `moveSelectedClipsToTrack` reroute to whole-group moves because moving doesn't break cohesion.
settled_decisions.rationale: Brief explicitly says only shot-label-menu Deconstruct/Delete may break a group. Moving is allowed because it preserves cohesion.

settled_decisions.decision: Single click selects the inner clip; cohesion enforced by write-path no-ops in the broader edit surface.
settled_decisions.rationale: No selection-level gating needed once mutations are blocked.

settled_decisions.decision: Shot-label-menu Delete shot preserves existing `handleDeleteClips(group.clipIds)` + `unpinGroup(...)` behavior. Unpin/Deconstruct calls only `unpinGroup`.
All three `PinnedShotGroup` clone sites (`migrate.ts:163`, `timeline-data.ts:91`, `usePinnedShotGroups.ts:31`) deep-copy `start`+`children`. Grep audit for ad-hoc clones.
Additive, idempotent in-place upgrade inside `repairConfig`; no schema version bump.
settled_decisions.rationale: On-disk config is per-project JSON; additive fields suffice.

settled_decisions.decision: `useExternalDrop` builds `children` inline using its existing `timeOffset` accumulator and `nextEdit.duration` inside the existing loop, then calls `buildGroupFromChildren`. It does NOT read `workingData.config.clips`. Selection-pinning uses the `buildGroupFromConfigClips` convenience wrapper.
settled_decisions.rationale: Resolves the pre-commit visibility problem without a second materialization step.

settled_decisions.decision: Shared `interactionStateRef: { drag, resize }` is a `useRef` declared in `TimelineEditor.tsx` (where all three consumers converge at lines 194, 479, 663-712) and passed via props/callbacks to `useClipDrag`, `TimelineCanvas`, and `usePinnedGroupSync`. NOT lifted into `useTimelineState.contexts.ts` or the split context surface. `useClipDrag` writes `.drag` on pointerdown-after-threshold/pointerup/cancel. `TimelineCanvas` `handleResizePointerDown`/`endResize` write `.resize` symmetric with `pendingOpsRef`. `usePinnedGroupSync`'s debounced callback reads `.drag || .resize` and re-arms the debounce when true.
settled_decisions.rationale: Smallest surface; no provider/type/test churn; matches the actual hook-graph convergence point.

orchestrator_guidance: Plan passed gate and preflight. Proceed to finalize. Verify unresolved flags against the plan and project code before accepting.

signals.idea:
Refactor pinned shot groups in the video editor to be first-class timeline entities, eliminating an entire class of "shot group disconnects" bugs that come from fragile parallel cohesion logic spread across drag, resize, sync, and render code paths.

## Why

The video editor's pinned shot groups (the colored bordered boxes that visually group N timeline clips under a single shot label) are currently a "soft" abstraction layered on top of flat `row.actions` arrays. The shot group is just a `{ shotId, trackId, clipIds: string[] }` record stored in `data.config.pinnedShotGroups`. Cohesion (the property that all the group's clips stay contiguous on the timeline) is enforced by SEPARATE bespoke logic in every code path that mutates clip positions:

- Drag: `useClipDrag` does "deferred expansion" — when the user grabs one clip in a group, it pulls the group's other clips into the drag set. Then `multi-drag-utils.applyMultiDragMoves` calls `resolveGroupOverlaps` which treats moved clips as one shifted group.
- Resize: `TimelineCanvas` resize handlers compute a `getShotGroupAdjacent` neighbor and write a paired `ResizeOverride` so the immediate neighbor follows the dragged edge.
- Sync: `usePinnedGroupSync` runs on a debounce after the underlying Shot's `images[]` changes; it rebuilds the group's clips one-by-one and shifts subsequent clips on the row.
- Track move / cross-track drag: updates `pinnedShotGroups[].trackId` via `updatePinnedShotGroupTrackIds`.
- Delete / unpin / "deconstruct" / "delete shot": each touches `pinnedShotGroups` differently.

There's no shared "the group is the unit of editing" primitive. Each path reinvents cohesion. Bugs keep emerging in different paths with the same user-visible symptom: "the shot disconnects."

On top of that, the visual contiguity check in `TimelineCanvas.tsx` (`positionedShotGroups`, around lines 446-499) iterates `group.clipIds` IN ARRAY ORDER and checks `current.start < previous.start || current.start - previous.end > snapThreshold`. **This assumes `group.clipIds` is in left-to-right spatial order, but nothing actively maintains that invariant.** `pinGroup` stores `[...selectedClipIds]` (Set iteration order, i.e. click order), and `usePinnedGroupSync` rebuilds in `desiredImages` order from the database. Once array order disagrees with spatial order by even one position, the group's border vanishes — user reports "disconnected."

We just shipped two patches for this in the drag and resize paths. The patches work for their cases but they don't address the root cause: the shot group is not a real entity in the editing model.

## Goal

Make a pinned shot group a FIRST-CLASS timeline entity. Conceptually:

- The shot group has its own bounds (`start`, `end`) on the row.
- It contains child clips with their own positions inside that bound (or relative offsets).
- Drag, resize, selection, and serialization all see the group as ONE thing by default.
- The contiguity invariant is enforced BY CONSTRUCTION, not by validation. Children of a group can never become non-contiguous because they live inside a single owning entity that lays them out.
- Operations on individual clips inside a group are explicit, intentional, and rare (the per-clip context menu is already locked down to asset-state actions only when a clip is in a pinned group, per a recent change).

## Hard requirements

1. **Eliminate the "shot disconnects" bug class.** After this refactor, no drag, resize, sync, or other normal interaction can make a pinned shot group's children become non-contiguous. The only way to break a group is the explicit "Deconstruct shot" or "Delete shot" actions on the shot label menu.

2. **No regression in user-facing behavior** for the supported flows:
   - Pinning a group from a selection.
   - Dragging a group on its row (snaps to bounds, snaps near siblings, can hit timeline start at t=0).
   - Dragging a group cross-track (entire group moves to the new row).
   - Resizing a group's outer left/right edge (stretches/shrinks the group, content reflows).
   - Resizing an interior boundary inside a group (between child clips) — re-evaluate whether this is even meaningful in the new model. If kept, must preserve cohesion.
   - The shot label menu actions (Deconstruct, Delete, Switch to Final Video, Switch to Images, Update to Latest Video, Generate Video, Jump to Shot).
   - Right-clicking a clip inside a pinned group still shows only asset-state actions per the recent change.
   - The `usePinnedGroupSync` debounce still keeps the group's children in sync with the underlying Shot's `images[]`.
   - Final video and image modes still work.
   - Auto-shift on resize from the recent megaplan still works (or is replaced by a better mechanism in the new model).

3. **Persistence stays compatible.** The on-disk timeline config schema currently stores `pinnedShotGroups: { shotId, trackId, clipIds, mode, imageClipSnapshot? }[]`. Decide whether the new model can keep this on-disk shape (with internal restructuring), or whether a schema migration is needed. If a migration is needed, it must be backward-compatible (old configs load correctly).

4. **Remotion preview rendering stays correct.** The Remotion compositions render flat clips. The refactor must not break what Remotion sees — i.e., individual clips inside a group must still be addressable for rendering, even if the editing model treats them as children of a group.

5. **Tests pass.** All existing tests in `src/tools/video-editor/` must continue to pass, OR be updated with clear justification when behavior intentionally changes. New tests should cover the cohesion-by-construction property.

## Design questions the plan must answer

The planning phase needs to investigate the codebase and propose answers to all of these. Do not guess — read the code:

1. **Data model.** Which of these is the right abstraction?
   a) "Group action" embedded in `row.actions`: the row's actions array becomes a heterogeneous list of `TimelineAction | GroupAction`, where `GroupAction` has its own start/end and a `children[]` of TimelineActions. Drag/resize see the GroupAction as a single thing.
   b) "Group resolver" overlay: keep `row.actions` as flat TimelineActions, but introduce a `GroupResolver` that, given a clipId or set of clipIds, returns the group bounds and member list. Drag/resize hooks consult the resolver and operate on the whole group when any member is touched.
   c) Something else.
   Trade off: (a) is conceptually cleaner but ripples through many types and Remotion assumptions; (b) is less invasive but still leaves cohesion as a soft property enforced by callers.

2. **Selection model.** When the user clicks one clip inside a group, do they select (i) the clip, (ii) the group, or (iii) is there a hover/click distinction (e.g., one click = group, double click = clip)? The current behavior is "click selects the clip"; the recent context-menu change limits what that clip can do. Pick a model that makes "you cannot accidentally break a group by editing one of its clips" obvious.

3. **Internal layout.** Inside a group, children are positioned. Are positions stored absolutely (each child has its own row-relative start/end) or relatively (each child has an offset from the group's start)? Relative positioning makes "shift the whole group by N seconds" trivial and impossible to break; absolute positioning means children can drift relative to each other (the original bug). Strongly prefer relative.

4. **Interior boundaries.** Today, a user can resize the boundary between two child clips of a group via the auto-shift mechanism we added. Decide whether interior boundary resize is still allowed in the new model. If yes, define it precisely. If no, document why not (likely it should not be — the user already said they don't really want per-clip operations inside a group).

5. **Sync interplay.** `usePinnedGroupSync` watches the Shot's `images[]` and rewrites the group's clips. In the new model, this becomes "rewrite the children of the group in place." Specify how this interacts with in-progress drags/resizes (it currently debounces; does that still work?).

6. **Migration / persistence.** Define the on-disk shape and the load-time conversion if needed. Old configs must load.

7. **Snap & timeline-start clamp.** Today the bug-fix from a few minutes ago lets `findBestGroupStart` clamp the group to t≥0 as a unit. In the new model, the group's `start` would be clamped directly. Make sure that still happens in every drag/resize path.

8. **Reuse of existing infrastructure.** `multi-drag-utils.ts`, `resolve-overlaps.ts`, `useClipDrag.ts`, `useClipResize.ts`, `usePinnedShotGroups.ts`, and `useShotGroups.ts` all encode pieces of cohesion. Decide which become obsolete and which get repurposed. Avoid leaving dead code.

## Files most likely to change

Start by reading these to ground the plan. Many more files will surface — explore from these as anchors:

- Data shape and config:
  - src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts (look for PinnedShotGroup, TimelineConfig)
  - src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts (resolve config -> TimelineData)
- Group accessors and sync:
  - src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.ts
  - src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usePinnedShotGroups.ts (pinGroup, unpinGroup, updatePinnedGroup, usePinnedGroupSync)
- Drag:
  - src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts (expandPinnedGroupDrag, deferredPinnedGroupClipIds)
  - src/tools/video-editor/lib/multi-drag-utils.ts (planMultiDragMoves, applyMultiDragMoves, updatePinnedShotGroupTrackIds)
  - src/tools/video-editor/lib/resolve-overlaps.ts (resolveGroupOverlaps, findBestGroupStart)
  - src/tools/video-editor/lib/snap-edges.ts
- Resize:
  - src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx (resize handlers, getShotGroupAdjacent, positionedShotGroups)
  - src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.ts (auto-shift transaction logic from the recent megaplan)
- Render and editor wiring:
  - src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx
  - src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx
  - src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ShotGroupContextMenu.tsx
- Provider / state:
  - src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.tsx
  - src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.types.ts
  - src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.contexts.ts
- Final video / image mode switching:
  - src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts (or similarly named)
- Remotion / preview:
  - Whatever in src/tools/video-editor/compositions/ and src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/ reads the timeline config -- verify it does not depend on flat row.actions in a way the new model breaks.

## Constraints

- Scope is the video-editor tool only. No DB schema changes, no edge functions, no migrations to Supabase. (The on-disk config is a JSON blob in the user's project file.)
- Follow the rules in CLAUDE.md at the repo root and in /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/CLAUDE.md.
- Prefer Edit over Write. No new docs unless explicitly necessary.
- Don't introduce new state management libraries or routing.
- Don't bundle unrelated cleanups. Keep the diff focused on the shot-group refactor.
- Pre-existing failing tests (one in TimelineCanvas.test.tsx about data-shot-group-kind="pinned") are unrelated and should be left alone.
- Reuse existing UI primitives in src/shared/components/ui/. No new color tokens.

## What success looks like

1. After the refactor, manually attempting to break a pinned shot group via any combination of normal drag, resize, snap, multi-select, or cross-track drag is impossible. The only way to break a group is the explicit Deconstruct/Delete actions.
2. Type check passes (npx tsc --noEmit).
3. Existing video-editor tests pass, plus new tests covering the cohesion-by-construction property.
4. The Remotion preview still renders the same output for an unchanged timeline.
5. The diff is concentrated in the video-editor tool with a clear before/after for the new abstraction.

Because `useClipDrag`, `TimelineCanvas`, and `usePinnedGroupSync` all already converge in `TimelineEditor`, pushing the new interaction state up into shared editor context broadens the implementation surface beyond what this repository structure appears to require.

Rev 3 says the ref should live in `useTimelineState.contexts.ts`, but that file only memoizes context values while `useClipDrag`, `TimelineCanvas`, and `usePinnedGroupSync` are all coordinated from `TimelineEditor`, so the stated location and rationale are still technically off.

Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: Checked the supporting infrastructure needed if rev 3 really exposes `interactionStateRef` and `isInteractionActive()` through the existing split editor contexts. Does the fix handle all of them?: Checked the `usePinnedGroupSync()` call site in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx:479` against the proposed context-based interaction guard. resolved_flags.concern: Timeline signatures: projecting grouped clip positions inside `assembleTimelineData()` would leave `resolvedConfig`, preview state, and `signature` stale unless the plan also rebuilds them from the projected config.
resolved_flags.resolution: Revised plan addresses 18 open flags by: (1) moving `projectPinnedShotGroups` upstream into `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry` so `resolvedConfig`/signature stay in sync, plus a commit-boundary projection in `useTimelineCommit.applyEdit` for `'config'`/`'pinnedShotGroups'` types; (2) updating all three clone helpers (`migrate.ts:163`, `timeline-data.ts:91`, `usePinnedShotGroups.ts:31`) to deep-copy `start`+`children`; (3) routing `useExternalDrop` shot-drop creation through `buildGroupFromClips`; (4) preserving Delete-shot's existing delete-then-unpin semantics (rev 1 had this wrong); (5) adding a new Step 11 that promotes clip-level mutations on group members (`useClipEditing` split/delete, `useKeyboardShortcuts` delete/split/Arrow keys, `useTimelineTrackManagement.moveSelectedClipsToTrack`) to group-level operations via a shared `categorizeSelection` helper; (6) explicit `isInteractionActive()` flag on `useDragCoordinator` so `usePinnedGroupSync` can defer mid-drag instead of relying on a non-existent guard; (7) added integration test at the commit boundary; (8) added `useExternalDrop.test.tsx` and `useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts` to the validation list. The hybrid model and settled decisions are preserved.

resolved_flags.concern: Pinned-group edit surface: Rev 2 now covers keyboard and clip-level mutation paths, but it still routes keyboard Delete on a selected group member to whole-shot deletion, which conflicts with the brief's requirement that only explicit shot-label-menu Deconstruct/Delete actions may break a group.
resolved_flags.resolution: Rev 3 fixes the 3 root causes the gate consolidated from rev 2's 16 open flags, none requiring redesign of the hybrid model: (1) **Keyboard delete/split on a group member is now a NO-OP** with an error toast, not a reroute to delete-shot semantics — the brief is explicit that only the shot-label menu may break a group. ArrowUp/Down and `moveSelectedClipsToTrack` still reroute to whole-group moves because moving doesn't break cohesion. (2) **`useExternalDrop` builds the group inside its existing loop** using the `timeOffset` accumulator and `nextEdit.duration` it already tracks at `useExternalDrop.ts:148-169` — a new `buildGroupFromChildren` primitive takes a `children` array directly so the helper doesn't need to read `workingData.config.clips` (which doesn't yet contain the new clip IDs). The selection-pinning caller uses a `buildGroupFromConfigClips` convenience wrapper. (3) **Interaction state is lifted into the editor state layer** (`useTimelineState.contexts.ts`) as a shared `interactionStateRef: { drag, resize }` ref, written by both `useClipDrag` (drag lifecycle) and `TimelineCanvas` resize handlers (`resizeSessionRef`/`pendingOpsRef` at `TimelineCanvas.tsx:737-843`), read by `usePinnedGroupSync`. Rev 2 wrongly assumed `useDragCoordinator` already tracked resizes. New Step 7 makes this explicit and Step 17 adds a sync-deferral test. All other rev-2 content (projection wiring, clone helpers, Delete-shot semantics preservation, broader edit surface) is preserved.

resolved_flags.concern: Shot-drop creation: Rev 2 names `useExternalDrop()`, but the proposed `buildGroupFromClips({ config: workingData.config, clipIds: createdClipIds, ... })` still cannot see freshly dropped clips because that path only mutates rows/meta/clipOrder until commit.

resolved_flags.concern: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: Checked the proposed shared `buildGroupFromClips` caller set against actual arguments. The selection-pin callers pass clip IDs that already exist in config, but the shot-drop caller in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts:148-181` would pass newly created clip IDs that are only present in rows/meta before commit, so one config-only helper signature does not actually handle both caller shapes as proposed.
resolved_flags.resolution: Rev 3 fixes the 3 root causes the gate consolidated from rev 2's 16 open flags, none requiring redesign of the hybrid model: (1) **Keyboard delete/split on a group member is now a NO-OP** with an error toast, not a reroute to delete-shot semantics — the brief is explicit that only the shot-label menu may break a group. ArrowUp/Down and `moveSelectedClipsToTrack` still reroute to whole-group moves because moving doesn't break cohesion. (2) **`useExternalDrop` builds the group inside its existing loop** using the `timeOffset` accumulator and `nextEdit.duration` it already tracks at `useExternalDrop.ts:148-169` — a new `buildGroupFromChildren` primitive takes a `children` array directly so the helper doesn't need to read `workingData.config.clips` (which doesn't yet contain the new clip IDs). The selection-pinning caller uses a `buildGroupFromConfigClips` convenience wrapper. (3) **Interaction state is lifted into the editor state layer** (`useTimelineState.contexts.ts`) as a shared `interactionStateRef: { drag, resize }` ref, written by both `useClipDrag` (drag lifecycle) and `TimelineCanvas` resize handlers (`resizeSessionRef`/`pendingOpsRef` at `TimelineCanvas.tsx:737-843`), read by `usePinnedGroupSync`. Rev 2 wrongly assumed `useDragCoordinator` already tracked resizes. New Step 7 makes this explicit and Step 17 adds a sync-deferral test. All other rev-2 content (projection wiring, clone helpers, Delete-shot semantics preservation, broader edit surface) is preserved.

resolved_flags.concern: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: Checked the keyboard delete caller in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.ts:108-110`. That caller only conveys 'delete the currently selected clip/member', not 'invoke shot-label Delete shot', so changing it to whole-shot deletion is a semantic change at the caller boundary rather than a transparent group-safety fix.
resolved_flags.resolution: Rev 3 fixes the 3 root causes the gate consolidated from rev 2's 16 open flags, none requiring redesign of the hybrid model: (1) **Keyboard delete/split on a group member is now a NO-OP** with an error toast, not a reroute to delete-shot semantics — the brief is explicit that only the shot-label menu may break a group. ArrowUp/Down and `moveSelectedClipsToTrack` still reroute to whole-group moves because moving doesn't break cohesion. (2) **`useExternalDrop` builds the group inside its existing loop** using the `timeOffset` accumulator and `nextEdit.duration` it already tracks at `useExternalDrop.ts:148-169` — a new `buildGroupFromChildren` primitive takes a `children` array directly so the helper doesn't need to read `workingData.config.clips` (which doesn't yet contain the new clip IDs). The selection-pinning caller uses a `buildGroupFromConfigClips` convenience wrapper. (3) **Interaction state is lifted into the editor state layer** (`useTimelineState.contexts.ts`) as a shared `interactionStateRef: { drag, resize }` ref, written by both `useClipDrag` (drag lifecycle) and `TimelineCanvas` resize handlers (`resizeSessionRef`/`pendingOpsRef` at `TimelineCanvas.tsx:737-843`), read by `usePinnedGroupSync`. Rev 2 wrongly assumed `useDragCoordinator` already tracked resizes. New Step 7 makes this explicit and Step 17 adds a sync-deferral test. All other rev-2 content (projection wiring, clone helpers, Delete-shot semantics preservation, broader edit surface) is preserved.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 3. Weighted score trajectory: 28.0 -> 27.0 -> 10.75. Plan deltas: 89.8%, 49.4%. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 16. Open significant flags: 7.

flag_resolutions.rationale: The critique is correct that lifting into `useTimelineState.contexts.ts` is broader than necessary. Execution guidance: declare the ref as a local `useRef<{drag: boolean; resize: boolean}>({drag: false, resize: false})` in `TimelineEditor.tsx` and pass it via props/callbacks to `useClipDrag`, `TimelineCanvas`, and `usePinnedGroupSync`. No context plumbing changes. The behavior contract is unchanged. This is a one-line location decision that the executor can make trivially; further planning iteration would be bikeshedding.

flag_resolutions.evidence: The concern is correct that `useTimelineState.contexts.ts:72-166` only memoizes context values, but the fix does not require planning iteration. `TimelineEditor.tsx:194,479,663-712` is where all three consumers converge, and a local `useRef` declared there with prop/callback wiring is the minimal correct implementation. The plan's *behavior* (drag writes, resize writes symmetric with `pendingOpsRef`, sync reads and re-arms debounce) is correct; only the stated physical location needs to change at execution time from the context layer to `TimelineEditor.tsx` local state. This is an executor-level decision, not a plan-level correctness bug.

The concern lists `useTimelineState.types.ts`, `TimelineEditorContext.tsx`, `VideoEditorProvider.tsx`, and `VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx` as required updates IF the ref goes through context. By moving the ref to a local `useRef` in `TimelineEditor.tsx` (per FLAG-006 resolution), none of those files need to change — the supporting infrastructure requirement disappears. The named files in the revised Step 7 (`useClipDrag.ts`, `TimelineCanvas.tsx`, plus `TimelineEditor.tsx` itself) are then sufficient.
